title: What‚Äôs my Late Processing Rate, and how do I keep it low?
bodyHtml:
The Late Processing Rate measures the percentage of orders that have been processed too late. It‚Äôs an important indicator of customer satisfaction, so keep a close eye on yours.

¬Ý

How is my Late Processing Rate calculated?

How do I keep my Late Processing Rate low?

‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

How is my Late Processing Rate calculated?

The Late Processing Rate measures the percentage of orders that have been processed too late. You‚Äôve got 48 hours to process your Trade-in order from the time the item is received. This timeline excludes weekends and bank holidays (in the country where you have your warehouse).

‚ÄúProcessing‚Äù the order means taking 1 of the following actions upon receiving the order:

  • Paying the customer the initial price offered
  • Making a counteroffer if you feel that the item's condition doesn't match how the customer assessed it (you‚Äôll need to provide photo or video evidence to back this up)
  • Putting the order on hold if the transaction can‚Äôt be completed due to a blocked account or missing information (such as a customer ID)

This is the Late Processing Rate formula: the number of Trade-in orders that were processed too late (after 48 hours) in the last 30 days, divided by the number of Trade-in orders received in the last 30 days. The Late Processing Rate is calculated every day.
¬Ý

What happens if my Late Processing Rate is too high?

If your Late Processing Rate is too high, you‚Äôll enter a ‚Äúmonitoring period" where you have the opportunity to improve your rate. If your rate doesn‚Äôt improve enough, your daily Trade-in limit will be decreased. If the issue persists, you won‚Äôt be able to use the Trade-in service.

‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚ÄãLearn more about Late Processing Rate limits and how the monitoring period works
¬Ý

How do I keep my Late Processing Rate low?

Here are some quick tips on processing your orders on time.

Check your Back Office frequently
Check your Back Office a few times a day to see if you‚Äôve received any new Trade-in items that need to be processed. It's quick and easy to see the Trade-in orders that still need processing by going to your Back Office > "Trade-in" > "Orders" > "New orders".

You'll also see a timer showing how much time you have left to process each order.

Make sure you‚Äôre getting email notifications
Make sure you‚Äôre receiving email notifications when an item has arrived at your chosen delivery location. If you‚Äôre not getting email notifications, reach out to the Seller Support Center to get them set up.

Make a plan to tackle big issues
If you‚Äôre anticipating or encountering an issue, consider the following options to solve it:

  • Adjust your process for handling orders received (including prioritisation of orders or a more organised workflow).
  • Review guidelines given to your employees.
  • Increase your resources temporarily.


Don‚Äôt forget to plan your holidays
If you‚Äôre planning to go on vacation or be away from your business, we suggest you update all your Trade-in order listings to ¬£0 about 2 weeks before your departure.

That said, you‚Äôll always need to process Trade-in order deliveries within 48 hours. Make sure you have the resources needed to process them while you‚Äôre away.
¬Ý

How should I monitor my performance indicators?

You can check the progress of your Late Processing Rate and other performance indicators in your Trade-in insights dashboard. You can access these from your Sales Back Office, then ‚ÄúInsights‚Äù > ‚ÄúTrade-in‚Äù.

If you have any questions, contact your Back Market point of contact or reach out via the Seller Support Center.
